label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what we know about the mother according to the text. The options are A to D. First, let me recall the story. The mother had five kids and was too busy raising them to have a garden. She used to say "Do you see a money tree outside?" when they wanted something expensive. Later, after the kids left, Marvin planted a tree with dollar bills and a note, and the other kids gave her gardening stuff. Now she has a garden and feels her kids are with her through the love. Looking at the options: A. She is a bad-tempered woman. Hmm. The text mentions she had to say the money tree line when kids asked for expensive things. But that's more about financial constraints, not necessarily bad temper. She seems loving, not bad-tempered. So probably not A. B. She believes money can grow on a tree now. The story ends with her saying she's not sure money grows on trees, but love does. So B is incorrect because she doesn't believe that now. C. She is a writer. The text doesn't mention her being a writer. The story is about her garden and kids. So C is probably wrong. D. She is hard-working in housekeeping. She had five kids, worked hard raising them, and later
